ticketTitle: Create a language-tier registry mapping every known language ID to an analysis capability tier (None/Generic/Lint/Parse/Full), with a test that fails CI when a new language extension is added to discovery without a tier entry.

nonGoals: Do NOT change scorer.go or integrate tiers into scoring
Do NOT modify init_cmd.go or report generation
Do NOT create an internal/language_tiers/ package
deliverables.objective: Create language-tier registry (Tier enum, static map of 22 language→tier, TierForLanguage helper) and exhaustive CI-enforcing test in internal/engine/
deliverables.planText:
Create internal/engine/language_tiers.go: define type Tier int with iota (TierNone=0, TierGeneric, TierLint, TierParse, TierFull) and a String() method. Define var languageTiers = map[string]Tier with all 22 known language IDs: go/ts/js/py/rs → TierFull; rb/java/kt/sh/c/cpp/cs/swift/php/elixir/erlang/scala/lua/r/zig/sql/proto → TierGeneric. Implement func TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier that returns the map value or TierGeneric for unknown keys.

Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go with three tests:
1. TestTierRegistry_AllKnownLanguagesHaveTiers — calls discovery.KnownLanguages(), asserts every returned ID maps to a non-zero Tier in languageTiers (fails CI when a new extension is added to certifiableExts without a registry entry)
2. TestTierRegistry_UnknownLanguageReturnsGeneric — asserts TierForLanguage("nonsense") == TierGeneric
3. TestTierRegistry_SwiftIsGeneric — asserts TierForLanguage("swift") == TierGeneric as a regression pin
